Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement
Off Calendar Jan-28-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
(650-638-2347) Marc Bender for Plaintiff/opposing party .

(925-944-3225) Patricia Peterson appearing for Defendant/moving party.

Court reporter:Angela Pourtabib. CSR # 13714. Telephone:(415) 601-7054. Email: apiurtabib@gmail.com

ARGUED. Ms. Peterson stated that there was no definitive statement, that the building the wall is implicit in the agreement, that the complaint states that it belongs to the plaintiff and that her client has suffered lose of use of the backyard. Mr. Bender reported that the wall has to be built soon, that cooperation is needed from the plaintiff and requested that the motion for summary judgment be moved to another date. The Court stated that it will not do anything orally.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as modified:
DEFENDANT DOUGLAS BAIRD Motion To Enforce Settlement is DENIED. Defendant moves for "an order enforcing the settlement in this action to compel Plaintiff to build a retaining wall." Nowhere in the Stipulation presented to the Court is there a definite obligation on the part of Plaintiff to build a wall. The parties did not come to the agreement as to "description of the process, time frames, and respective responsibilities for accomplishing the repairs." The parties agreed in relevant parts as to (1) the responsibility for payment of the costs (which has not yet ripened as no costs are alleged to have been incurred) and also agreed (2) to enter into a more detailed agreement that would include the responsibilities of the parties (presumably determining who is responsible for the "leg work" of hiring professionals, obviating permits, obtaining consent to the other side, providing notice of entry and commencement of work, etc.). These are material terms which have to be agreed upon before the Court may consider ordering a party to "build a retaining wall" pursuant to stipulation. It appears that parties attempted to reach an agreement as provided for in paragraph 7. October 18, 2017 Agreement is not enforceable in the manner sought by the Defendant. Plaintiff's request for attorney's fees is granted in the amount of $1,452.50 (fees incurred in connection with the "enforcement" of the agreement; all other fees listed were incurred in accordance with the terms of the agreement). Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b).

Per stipulation of counsel, Defendant's unopposed motion for Summary Judgment (taken off calendar pursuant to paragraph 2 of the Stipulation) will be restored to calendar and will be heard on February 27, 2019 (placing the action in the same procedural position it was before the Settlement Agreement was entered into). No additional briefing is allowed.

Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
